tavily.answer: In 2025, the number of internal displacements caused by conflict or violence reached a record high, surpassing those caused by natural disasters for the first time, according to a report by the Internal Displacement Monitoring Centre. In the Democratic Republic of the Congo, efforts to combat the Ebola outbreak have been complicated by the presence of internally displaced persons in camps with limited resources. The United Nations’ food agencies have warned that acute hunger is set to worsen across 13 global hotspots, including Sudan, South Sudan, Yemen, Palestine, Nigeria, and Somalia, due to conflict, funding shortages, and climate shocks. Uganda has closed its border with the Democratic Republic of the Congo to contain the spread of the Ebola outbreak, which has already crossed borders and killed more than 220 people.
